expectations.text: card() function returns Dom.Element as reusable component
expectations.evidence: Lines 23-49: Function signature returns Dom.Element, implementation returns div(...) element properly wrapping card structure

expectations.text: Component accepts varargs (Dom*) for flexible children content
expectations.evidence: Line 27: Function parameter (content: Dom*): Dom.Element; Line 43: div(class := "card-body") accepts content variable containing all passed elements

expectations.text: Optional title parameter with Option[String] type uses Option.map pattern
expectations.evidence: Lines 24, 36-39: title: Option[String] = None; title.map(t => div(class := "card-header")(h5(...)(t)))

expectations.text: Optional footer parameter with Option[Dom] type uses Option.map pattern
expectations.evidence: Lines 25, 46-48: footer: Option[Dom] = None; footer.map(f => div(class := "card-footer")(f))

expectations.text: Conditional className based on 'starred' parameter, adds 'card-highlighted' class correctly
expectations.evidence: Lines 28-33: className := (if (starred) ("card", "card-highlighted") else ("card",)) - properly uses conditional logic to add second class when starred is true

expectations.text: Alternative .when() pattern demonstrated for conditional attributes (cardWithWhen function)
expectations.evidence: Lines 55-76: cardWithWhen uses .when(starred)(className := ("card", "card-highlighted")) pattern demonstrating recommended approach for conditional class addition
